title: cambessedesia adamantium
side_effects: Cambessedesia adamantium can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing severe nausea, dizziness, and headaches in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to its extracts can lead to liver damage and kidney failure, as well as increased risk of cardiac arrest. In rare cases, improper use of the plant can also result in neurological disorders, including seizures and memory loss. Furthermore, it can also interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions such as diabetes and high blood pressure.
